html:
Exclusive to:

“A record of all the foods cooked and/or eaten, as well as the ingredients used to cook them.”

-

For complete list of Crock Pot recipes, see Dishes.
For complete list of Portable Crock Pot recipes, see Portable Crock Pot.


The Cookbook is a Cooking Filter item exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It requires 1 Papyrus and 1 Carrot to craft and a Science Machine to prototype.

If the player is the one who cooked it, the player learns a recipe after picking the food item from either the Crock Pot or the Portable Crock Pot. Learned recipes are stored in the player's online profile and are conserved between all servers. They can be viewed in the Compendium. The Cookbook is not needed to learn recipes, but it is needed to display the acquired knowledge.

Once the food is eaten, it shows the amount of Health, Sanity, and Hunger the recipe changes, as well as bonus effects, such as Jellybeans' regeneration effect, or Spicy Chili's temperature increase.

The Cookbook recipes can be sorted in 5 categories: Alphabetic, Health, Sanity, Hunger and Side Effects. "Alphabetic" will show the recipes in alphabetic order. Choosing any stat will show the recipes you know that heal the most of the selected one first. Choosing "Side Effect" will show recipes with bonus effects first. You may also filter the recipes as: Meat, Veggie, Others, and Side Effect. Choosing any of the options will show only recipes with the criteria.

The following tables below shows the correspondence between the information in the cookbook and the actual values.

Spoil Time Cookbook
≤ 6 days Quickly
≤ 10 days Average
≤ 15 days Slowly
> 15 days Very Slowly
Never Never
CookTime Cookbook
< 20s Short
< 40s Average
< 50s Long
≥ 50s Very Long

Trivia

  • The Cookbook was introduced in the August QOL Update.
  • Modded foods are saved locally. They are not saved to player's online profile, but they do appear in their own tab within the cookbook screen.
  • The Cookbook layout is very similar to the one in The Gorge event.
  • Before the Reap What You Sow update, a cookbook's cost was 1 papyrus, 1 feather pencil, and 2 silk.

Gallery

  • Cookbook layout as seen ingame.
  • Chef's Specials page in Cookbook. Unlocked as Warly using Portable Crockpot.
  • Cookbook on a promo for the August 2020 Quality of Life update.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

Exclusive to:
“I've always been hungry for knowledge.”
–Wilson
“Uuugh, it's no fun cooking with instructions!”
–Willow
“Is book of things to fill Wolfgang's mighty belly!”
–Wolfgang
“What's the point of filling my stomach when my heart remains empty...”
–Wendy
“DIRECTORY OF INGESTIBLE ORGANIC MATTER COMBINATIONS”
–WX-78
“A collection of culinary observations.”
–Wickerbottom
“Pretty handy. Luce, how aboot you read off the ingredients while I cook?”
–Woodie
“Very well. If I can master the dark arts, how hard could cooking be?”
–Maxwell
“'Tis filled with töo many nön-meat dishes if yöu ask me.”
–Wigfrid
“Now we'll never forget a recipe!”
–Webber
“I added a few of my own personal recipes.”
–Warly
“Belly things”
–Wormwood
“Havin' a blueprint sure makes cooking easier!”
–Winona
“Oh what shall I cook up next? Hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Like this one, has pictures of food!”
–Wurt
“Looks like I've got a new guide to memorize!”
–Walter
“Cooking is so time consuming, look at all these ingredients and methods!”
–Wanda
“A record of all the foods cooked and/or eaten, as well as the ingredients used to cook them.”

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Sách dạy nấu ăn là một vật phẩm Bộ lọc nấu ăn dành riêng cho Don't Starve Together. Nó cần 1 Giấy cói và 1 Cà rốt để chế tạo và Máy Khoa học để tạo nguyên mẫu.

The '''Cookbook''' is a [[Cooking Filter]] item exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. It requires 1 [[Papyrus]] and 1 [[Carrot]] to craft and a [[Science Machine]] to prototype.

If the player is the one who cooked it, the player learns a recipe after picking the food item from either the [[Crock Pot]] or the [[Portable Crock Pot]]. Learned recipes are stored in the player's online profile and are conserved between all servers. They can be viewed in the [[Compendium]]. The Cookbook is not needed to learn recipes, but it is needed to display the acquired knowledge. 

Once the food is eaten, it shows the amount of [[Health]], [[Sanity]], and [[Hunger]] the recipe changes, as well as bonus effects, such as [[Jellybeans]]' regeneration effect, or [[Spicy Chili]]'s temperature increase.

The Cookbook recipes can be sorted in 5 categories: Alphabetic, Health, Sanity, Hunger and Side Effects. "Alphabetic" will show the recipes in alphabetic order. Choosing any stat will show the recipes you know that heal the most of the selected one first. Choosing "Side Effect" will show recipes with bonus effects first. You may also filter the recipes as: Meat, Veggie, Others, and Side Effect. Choosing any of the options will show only recipes with the criteria.

The following tables below shows the correspondence between the information in the cookbook and the actual values.

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The Cookbook was introduced in the [[Don't_Starve_Together/Version_History#August_20.2C_2020_-_August_QOL_Update|August QOL Update]].
* Modded foods are saved locally. They are not saved to player's online profile, but they do appear in their own tab within the cookbook screen.
*The Cookbook layout is very similar to the one in ''[[The Gorge]]'' event. 
*Before the ''[[Reap What You Sow]]'' update, a cookbook's cost was 1 papyrus, 1 feather pencil, and 2 silk.
